key_questions: Geographic risk: known Lyme-endemic area (Southern Ontario, BC, NS, NB, QC, MB)?
Tick species: black-legged tick (deer tick, Ixodes scapularis) — the vector for Lyme in Canada?
Tick attachment time: ≥36h attached = significant transmission risk (must be engorged)
Time since tick removal: <72h = prophylaxis window
Ability to identify tick (black-legged vs dog tick)?
red_flags: Bull's eye rash (erythema migrans, ≥5 cm expanding) → early Lyme disease — requires treatment not just prophylaxis
Flu-like symptoms (fever, fatigue, myalgia, headache, neck stiffness) after tick bite → treat for Lyme
Neurological symptoms or cardiac symptoms (palpitations, syncope) → disseminated Lyme — urgent refer
Rash at any other body site after tick bite in endemic area
ddx: Rocky Mountain spotted fever (rare in Canada)
Local allergic reaction to tick bite

first_line.name: Doxycycline Prophylaxis (single dose)
first_line.drugs: Doxycycline 200 mg single oral dose (adults) | 4.4 mg/kg single dose (children ≥8 years, max 200 mg)
first_line.notes: Indication: all 4 criteria must be met — (1) black-legged tick (Ixodes scapularis) confirmed; (2) attached ≥36h; (3) in endemic area (check Ontario Lyme disease risk map — Public Health Ontario); (4) prophylaxis started within 72h of tick removal. Efficacy: 87% reduction in Lyme disease if criteria met. Take with food (GI side effects). Avoid in pregnancy (<8 weeks) and children <8 years.
first_line.preferred_when: All 4 prophylaxis criteria met (tick species + attachment time + endemic area + within 72h)

second_line.name: Watchful Waiting (if criteria not fully met)
second_line.drugs: No prophylaxis — monitor for symptoms × 30 days
second_line.notes: If prophylaxis criteria not all met (dog tick, <36h attached, non-endemic area): observe only. Advise patient to return immediately if erythema migrans rash or flu-like symptoms develop within 30 days.

non_pharm: Proper tick removal: fine-tipped tweezers, grasp close to skin surface, pull upward steadily — no twisting, no petroleum jelly/fire
Save the tick (sealed container or tape to paper) for identification
Report tick bite to public health if in endemic area
Prevention: light-coloured clothing, long pants/sleeves in wooded areas, DEET insect repellent (20–30%), check for ticks after outdoor activity
treatment.refer_when: Erythema migrans rash (expanding bull's eye, ≥5 cm); flu-like illness after tick bite; neurological or cardiac symptoms; child <8 years requiring prophylaxis; pregnancy; prophylaxis criteria not met but uncertain
